user_prompt: this one uses the newer ExtCodec, and it starts up with the correct sequence, I2S setup before I2C setup
use a lookup table for the sine wave
feed the I2S using the existing pump mechanisms, like the `test_speaker` test

I'm going to try wiring the headphone jack on the SGTL5000 into a cheap USB mic adapter with a 3.5mm mic input, see what the signal looks like. So only make the sine wave like 1/4 total allowed amplitude
agent_reply: It does the SGTL5000-safe startup order:

The sine is a 440 Hz lookup-table oscillator, queued into `bluetoothToSpeakerFifo()` and fed out with `AudioManager::pump_bt2spk()`, matching the existing `test_speaker` mechanism. PCM amplitude is `+/-8191`, about 1/4 full-scale int16. The external codec speaker path disables the NS4168 path.
I also registered it in [all_tests.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h:34) and made it the active test in [all_tests.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp:30).
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: build passed. Only the existing FTP deprecated `available()` warning showed up.

user_prompt: where does it disable the NS4168?
agent_reply: It happens inside the external-codec speaker path, not directly in the test.
Then [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:232) chooses the external codec path:
And that helper writes the PMIC GPIO controlling the NS4168 power/enable at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1248).
It happens inside the external-codec speaker path, not directly in the test.
Then [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:232) chooses the external codec path:
And that helper writes the PMIC GPIO controlling the NS4168 power/enable at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1248).
